content.problemStatement:
We define the goodness of any array BBB of length MMM as: ‚ąĎi=1M‚ąí1‚ą£Bi+1‚ąíBi‚ą£\sum_{i = 1}^{M - 1} |B_{i + 1} - B_{i}|‚ąĎi=1M‚ąí1‚Äč‚ą£Bi+1‚Äč‚ąíBi‚Äč‚ą£ (Here ‚ą£X‚ą£|X|‚ą£X‚ą£ denotes the absolute value of XXX). In particular, goodness of any array of length 111 is 000.

Alice and Bob have an array AAA containing NNN **distinct** elements and they play a game with it.

Alice starts the game after which both the players make moves alternatively. In one move:


A player can **delete** any subarray from AAA such that the goodness of AAA does **not** change.
Formally, the player chooses indices LLL and RRR such that 1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§length(A)1 \le L \le R \le \mathrm{length}(A)1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§length(A) and removes the subarray [AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äč] from AAA such that the goodness of AAA does not change.

For e.g. A=[3,1,2,4]A = [3, 1, 2, 4]A=[3,1,2,4], a player can select L=3,R=3L = 3, R = 3L=3,R=3, then the resulting array will be [3,1,4][3, 1, 4][3,1,4]. Initially, the goodness was ‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£=5|1-3| + |2-1| + |4-2| = 5‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£=5. After the move, the goodness is ‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí1‚ą£=5|1-3| + |4-1| = 5‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí1‚ą£=5. Here we can observe that the **goodness** does not change.

The player who is not able to make a move loses. If both the players play optimally, determine who out of Alice and Bob will win the game.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the winner of the game if both players play optimally.


You may print each character of `Alice` and `Bob` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `aLiCe`, `ALIce`, `alice` will be considered identical).
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Alice can not make any moves such that the goodness of the array does not change. Therefore Bob wins.
Test case 2: Alice can choose L=2,R=3L = 2, R = 3L=2,R=3 and remove the subarray [A2,A3][A_2, A_3][A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč]. The resulting array is [1,4][1, 4][1,4].

Goodness of the array before the operation =‚ą£2‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£3‚ąí2‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí3‚ą£=3 = |2-1|+|3-2|+|4-3| = 3=‚ą£2‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£3‚ąí2‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí3‚ą£=3.
Goodness of the array after the operation =‚ą£4‚ąí1‚ą£=3 = |4-1| = 3=‚ą£4‚ąí1‚ą£=3.

Thus, the array has the same goodness as before. Now Bob can not make any move. Therefore Alice wins.
